generations.code:
Row 1: . . X X X
Row 2: . . . X .
Row 3: . . . X .
Row 4: . . . X .

bugs.description: Off‑by‑one error in the prefix‑sum check for "bad" column pairs. The implementation treats column i as bad when there exists a row r with a[r][i] == 'X' and a[r-1][i-1] == 'X', but the query condition should consider columns (l+1 … r) inclusive. The code mistakenly includes column l in the check, causing false "NO" answers for queries where the leftmost column participates in a bad pair.

Missing handling of the single‑row edge case (n == 1). The algorithm assumes that a "bad" pair can only appear when there are at least two rows, but when n == 1 any column containing an 'X' is trivially non‑exitable and the determinability condition reduces to checking for adjacent 'X' cells in the same row. The current code never marks any column as bad, leading to "YES" for all queries, which is incorrect for grids like "X.X".

Incorrect initialization of the "bad" array for the first column. The solution sets bad[1] = 0 by default, but if there is an 'X' in column 1 and also an 'X' directly below it (row r>1), the subgrid consisting only of column 1 is not determinable because the exitability of the lower cell cannot be distinguished from a filled cell. The code never flags column 1 as bad, causing "YES" for queries (1,1) on such grids.

bugs.description: Off‑by‑one error in the prefix‑sum check for "bad" column pairs. The implementation treats column i as bad when there exists a row r with a[r][i] == 'X' and a[r-1][i-1] == 'X', but the query condition should consider columns (l+1 … r) inclusive. The code mistakenly includes column l in the check, causing false "NO" answers for queries where the leftmost column participates in a bad pair.

Missing handling of the single‑row edge case (n == 1). The algorithm assumes that a "bad" pair can only appear when there are at least two rows, but when n == 1 any column containing an 'X' is trivially non‑exitable and the determinability condition reduces to checking for adjacent 'X' cells in the same row. The current code never marks any column as bad, leading to "YES" for all queries, which is incorrect for grids like "X.X".

Incorrect initialization of the "bad" array for the first column. The solution sets bad[1] = 0 by default, but if there is an 'X' in column 1 and also an 'X' directly below it (row r>1), the subgrid consisting only of column 1 is not determinable because the exitability of the lower cell cannot be distinguished from a filled cell. The code never flags column 1 as bad, causing "YES" for queries (1,1) on such grids.
